title: gravesia macrophylla var. subcordata
intro: Gravesia macrophylla var. subcordata, also known as Gravesia, is a plant species that belongs to the family Celastraceae and is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory activities. Gravesia is believed to have health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating infections, and it is often used to prepare herbal remedies for these purposes. In traditional herbal preparations, Gravesia is typically used in the form of a decoction or infusion, where the leaves or roots of the plant are boiled or steeped in hot water to release their active compounds.
